seo.title: Pork belly - gritssssss crackling - Marinda Kook

html:
• If you want to cook the little pig, you mustn't be in a hurry. He needs to cook slowly in his dish. The secret of good glass-crack crackling I'll tell you now. Set your oven to 160 degrees Celsius and know it's going to take 4 hours to cook.
  1. pork belly
  2. 2 x bottles Savanna beer
  3. 8 sage leaves
  4. coarse salt
  5. baking powder
  6. 4 onions sliced into rings
  7. 6 cloves
  8. 750 ml water
  9. 6 large onions (to add later)
  10. 6 potatoes (to add later)

NOW

  • in a roasting dish put your onions, cider, sage leaves and water
  • put your pork belly on top and press the cloves in
  • now rub the pork belly first with baking powder and then with coarse salt (like you would rub powder into a baby's bottom)

This is now where patience comes into play. Put your dish uncovered in the oven. Peek in now and then to see if there's enough liquid in your dish. If it gets low, top it up with water.

After the little pig has been in the oven for 2 hours, add your whole potatoes and onions. Turn them regularly and they'll turn golden brown.

After another 2 hours your little pig is cooked. Slice the pork and arrange it on a nice plate. Pour the sauce into a pot and thicken it with Bistro on the stove top. Enjoy the deliciousness and listen to your crackling crack.
